clusters.headline: South Africa: Parties Split Over Ramaphosa Impeachment Process - South African News Briefs - July 23, 2026
India’s Modi promises fast-track courts for exam fraud fuelling protests
clusters.summary: Cockroach Janta Party protesters are occupying streets in New Delhi, demanding Education Minister Pradhan resign.

clusters.headline: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
clusters.summary: The two, a political consultant and a translator, denied allegations of fomenting protests last year.

clusters.headline: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
clusters.summary: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

clusters.headline: Africa: Germany's Growing Interest in Africa - A Win for Both Sides?
clusters.summary: [DW] Germany is deepening its footprint across Africa, as Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul embarks on his third trip to the continent in 2026. His top-level talks will focus on security, skills development and investment.

clusters.headline: IRGC claims several attacks on US army facilities in Kuwait
Fire kills 10 members of same family in Peru, police say
clusters.summary:
Authorities are investigating the cause of the blaze in Lima as local media report
 it may have been arson.

clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: From Lithium to Nickel - How the UN Is Helping Countries Make the Most of Critical Minerals
clusters.summary: [UN News] The world's shift to clean energy is driving huge demand for critical minerals such as lithium, nickel, cobalt and copper.

clusters.headline: Commentary: PM Wong’s Cabinet reshuffle shows his focus on building Singapore’s next generation of leaders
clusters.summary: The prime minister’s challenge will be to battle-test the rookies before the veterans eventually step back, says former Nominated Member of Parliament Nicholas Fang.

clusters.headline: Madagascar: Alarm in Madagascar Over Wave of Unresolved Child and Adolescent Disappearances and Killings
clusters.summary: [Agenzia Fides] Antananarivo -- Alarm is growing in Madagascar over a wave of unresolved disappearances and killings that, since the beginning of the year, has particularly affected children and adolescents, especially in and around the capital, Antananarivo.

clusters.headline: Japanese firm transferred S$6 million in email impersonation scam; director of recipient company jailed
clusters.summary: Rian Rahim agreed to help set up a company that would purportedly sell toys imported from China, but its bank account was later used to receive scam proceeds.

clusters.headline: Xi Jinping warns of ‘new historical injustice’ in AI. What does he mean - and can it be prevented?
clusters.summary: The warning by China’s president draws on a long-standing domestic narrative about the costs of falling behind in previous technological revolutions, say analysts.
